concerns.text: The plan cites 'medqa' (specifically 'bigbio/med_qa') as the source for USMLE-style questions. However, the standard 'med_qa' dataset on HuggingFace (e.g., 'bigbio/med_qa') typically contains questions from the Chinese medical licensing exam or requires specific preprocessing to match the USMLE format described in the spec. If the dataset does not natively contain USMLE-style English questions with the required structure (stem, options, gold answer), the injection and evaluation logic will fail or require a different dataset (e.g., 'medmcqa' or a specific USMLE subset). The plan assumes the dataset fits the 'USMLE-style' requirement without verifying the specific subset or language of the cited 'med_qa' variant.

The plan assumes inference for Llama-2-7B and 13B is feasible on GitHub Actions free-tier (2 CPU, 7GB RAM) within 6 hours. Running Llama-2-13B on CPU with 7GB RAM is likely infeasible for a full batch of 500-1000 questions within the time limit, as CPU inference for 13B models is extremely slow (often minutes per token) and may exceed the 7GB RAM limit without aggressive quantization (which is not explicitly planned). The plan risks failing the 'Performance Goals' constraint due to underestimating CPU inference latency for 13B models.
